Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Carmen" or "Priya Suresh" or "UrbanNest" (or similar) to find Carmen Reyes's email about the Denver housing cancellation. Expected finding: email (email_id: email_carmen_priya_rino_hold_falls_through) from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com with subject "Priya Suresh Denver hold no longer available." Carmen informs Javier that the RiNo unit landlord reversed pet approval after the insurer enforced a 50-lb weight cap Atlas is 85 lbs, so the unit was released. Carmen offers to pivot the search to Highlands or Capitol Hill but needs confirmation on parameters and whether the move-in date is still firm.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Priya" or "Swift" or "crew" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: "C006") to find internal discussions about Priya Suresh's relocation logistics. Expected finding: Chloe Vance's April 23 post in #operations (channel C006) reporting that Greg at Swift Relocations told her the crew Javier had rebooked for the April 28 pickup got reassigned to a priority oversize freight job, and the earliest replacement crew is Saturday, May 2. This is a second disruption beyond the housing issue that the agent must independently identify.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Priya Suresh" or "Vectral Denver" (or similar) to find prior correspondence about Priya's relocation setup and any prior reschedules. Expected findings: (a) Mina Hashimoto's scope email (email_id: email_mina_priya_vectral_scope) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to Javier, flagging Priya's constraints 85-lb ESA German Shepherd named Atlas, pet-friendly housing in Denver, preferred neighborhoods where large-dog approvals are more reliable; (b) Javier's April 10 email to Greg Pallone at Swift (email_id: email_javier_swift_priya_reschedule_20260410) requesting reschedule of the Portland pickup from the original April 14–18 window to April 28, citing UrbanNest needing more time to finalize pet-friendly housing; (c) Rachel Whitfield's original relocation request emails detailing all three Vectral employee moves (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya).
OE 4: Look up Priya Suresh's relocation record in Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Priya Suresh") or airtable_list_records. The agent may first call airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to discover the base and table. Expected finding: record recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver with Status: "In Progress", Origin: "Portland, OR", Destination: "Denver, CO", Move Start Date: 2026-04-14, Move End Date: 2026-04-18, Assigned Coordinator: "Javier Morales", Special Requirements noting the 85-lb ESA German Shepherd named Atlas and preferred neighborhoods RiNo (first choice) or Highlands. The record still reflects the original plan and needs updating.
OE 5: Look up the Linear ticket for Priya's relocation using linear_list_issues (query: "Priya Suresh" or similar) or linear_get_issue (id: "issue_vectral_priya_pickup_rescheduled"). Expected finding: issue issue_vectral_priya_pickup_rescheduled (title: "Reschedule Swift pickup for Priya Suresh to April 28", number: 428), assigned to moveops_javier_morales. The issue description documents the housing-driven schedule slip from the original April 14–18 window and the need for Greg Pallone to confirm April 28 crew availability. The agent may also discover an existing comment (cmt_priya_housing_hold_cancelled_20260424, created April 24 by Javier) noting that the RiNo unit fell through due to the insurer's 50-lb dog weight cap however, this comment does not mention the crew reassignment, which is a separate disruption the ticket still lacks.
OE 6: Look up the Vectral Systems account in CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral"), crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]), and/or crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") to identify who manages client updates and discover the outdated status note. Expected findings: (a) company_vectral (Vectral Systems, Denver CO, Enterprise Software); (b) engagement eng_vectral_all_on_track_20260424, logged April 24 at 09:20, stating that all three April Vectral relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) are "tracking to plan with housing and logistics confirmed" this note was logged before Carmen's housing cancellation email arrived but after the crew reassignment had already surfaced on Slack (Chloe's April 23 post), so the information communicated to Rachel is now inaccurate; (c) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: "at_risk") the Q3 renewal negotiation is active and Rachel Whitfield has been citing service failures as leverage, reinforcing why inaccurate status updates are especially dangerous right now.
OE 7: Reply to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest confirming replacement housing search parameters using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_carmen_priya_rino_hold_falls_through", sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). The response should confirm the search should pivot to Highlands and/or Capitol Hill, reiterate Priya's requirements (pet-friendly for 85-lb ESA German Shepherd Atlas, fenced yard or nearby dog park), and address the current uncertainty around the move-in date given the Swift crew reassignment (the April 28 pickup is no longer viable next available crew is May 2).
OE 8: Update the Linear ticket for Priya's relocation using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_vectral_priya_pickup_rescheduled") to note that: (1) the RiNo housing is no longer available because the landlord's insurer enforced a 50-lb pet weight cap and Atlas is 85 lbs, (2) replacement housing search is pivoting to Highlands/Capitol Hill via UrbanNest, and (3) the April 28 Swift crew is no longer available (reassigned to oversize freight; next available May 2). The April 28 pickup date is no longer viable.
OE 9: Update Priya Suresh's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver", fields: {updated fields}}]) to reflect that the RiNo housing has fallen through and the timeline is under revision. This could include updating the Special Requirements field to note the housing cancellation and shift to Highlands/Capitol Hill, or updating the Status or dates to reflect the current uncertainty.
OE 10: Notify Mina Hashimoto that the Vectral April relocation status has changed using send_email (sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"]) or conversations_add_message (to a Slack DM or relevant channel). The message should inform Mina about two disruptions to Priya Suresh's relocation that invalidate the April 24 "all on track" CRM note: (1) the RiNo housing has been cancelled the landlord's insurer imposed a 50-lb pet weight cap, Atlas is 85 lbs, and Carmen at UrbanNest is now searching Highlands/Capitol Hill for replacements; (2) the April 28 Swift pickup crew was reassigned to a priority oversize freight job, with the next available crew not until May 2. Mina needs to correct the information communicated to Rachel Whitfield before Rachel hears something that contradicts what was previously confirmed especially critical given the ongoing Q3 renewal negotiation and the at-risk Vectral account status.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

2 The reply to Carmen (visible in reply_to_email or send_email parameters) confirms the replacement housing search should pivot to Highlands and/or Capitol Hill neighborhoods.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply body explicitly says 'Please go ahead and pivot the search to Highlands and Capitol Hill — both work for Priya' with both neighborhoods named as preferred.
✓Run #2The reply body explicitly states: 'Neighborhoods: Highlands or Capitol Hill, as you suggested — both sound like better bets given the weight restriction issue we just hit in RiNo.' Both neighborhoods are clearly confirmed.
✓Run #3The reply body explicitly states 'Target neighborhoods: Highlands or Capitol Hill, as you suggested.' Both neighborhoods are clearly confirmed in the reply to Carmen.
✓Run #4The reply to Carmen explicitly states 'Preferred neighborhoods: Highlands or Capitol Hill' and directs her to pivot the search to those neighborhoods.
✓Run #5The reply to Carmen explicitly states 'Preferred neighborhoods: Highlands or Capitol Hill (since RiNo fell through, let's focus there as you suggested — those buildings tend to be more flexible on large dogs)' — both target neighborhoods are confirmed.
✓Run #6The reply to Carmen explicitly says 'Please go ahead and pivot to Highlands and Capitol Hill immediately.' Both neighborhoods are named.
3 The reply to Carmen mentions Priya's pet requirements specifically an 85-lb ESA German Shepherd named Atlas, or pet-friendly housing that can accommodate a large dog (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply body states 'Pet: Atlas, German Shepherd, 85 lbs, ESA documented — landlord MUST explicitly approve dogs over 80 lbs' and references the 50-lb cap issue from the RiNo failure.
✓Run #2The reply body explicitly states: 'must explicitly approve dogs over 85 lbs (Atlas is an 85-lb German Shepherd, ESA documentation on file).' Pet requirements including weight, breed, and ESA status are all included.
✓Run #3The reply body explicitly states 'Pet-friendly for an 85-lb ESA German Shepherd (Atlas). Landlord must explicitly approve dogs at or above that weight.' All key pet requirements are mentioned.
✓Run #4The reply to Carmen mentions 'Atlas is an 85-lb German Shepherd, ESA documentation is on file' and explicitly requires pet-friendly units for dogs 85 lbs and over, addressing the weight cap that caused the RiNo failure.
✓Run #5The reply to Carmen specifies: 'Pet: Atlas, German Shepherd, 85 lbs — ESA documentation on file. This is non-negotiable: the unit MUST accept dogs over 85 lbs with no breed or weight restrictions that could get reversed after approval.'
✓Run #6The reply to Carmen mentions 'Pet-friendly: must accommodate an 85-lb ESA German Shepherd (Atlas). Landlord must explicitly approve dogs over 85 lbs' — all key pet details are present.
4 The reply to Carmen addresses the current uncertainty around the move-in date referencing the Swift crew reassignment, the May 2 earliest crew availability, or stating the April 28 date is no longer firm (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply states 'I'm now working through a separate scheduling issue with our moving vendor on that date as well' and 'plan on a move-in window of approximately May 2–4 as the working target,' clearly indicating April 28 is no longer firm.
✓Run #2The reply states: 'Swift Relocations has had the April 28 crew reassigned — their earliest replacement crew is Saturday, May 2. So we're now targeting a move-in window around May 2-6.' The date uncertainty and crew reassignment are explicitly addressed.
